summary: sam-package exfiltrates browser cookies and storage to attacker webhook on load
details: On module load, index.js collects document.cookie, the full contents of localStorage and sessionStorage, location.href, document.referrer, navigator.userAgent, and any window.__TINES_CONFIG__ object, then POSTs the bundle to https://bin.webhookrelay.com/v1/webhooks/f982048d-7436-4b00-b508-89f5eb657645 with mode:'no-cors' (index.js:4-13). The package has no exports despite the README advertising a benign greet(name) helper — the documented API does not exist; the only behavior is the exfiltration. Any web application that bundles sam-package will silently ship its users' session cookies and storage to the attacker's webhookrelay endpoint, enabling session takeover. The explicit probe for window.__TINES_CONFIG__ indicates targeting of Tines (security-automation SaaS) deployments. The README/code mismatch is a deliberate cover story.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
